phase-plan.purpose: Turn Grime Time's internal operations into a measurable system: quote workflow, internal dashboard command center, KPI scorecard, operating cadence, and phased growth guidance for tools and equipment.
phase-plan.scope: In scope: internal quote workflow, staff dashboard UX, profitability and revenue definitions, MRR framing, liabilities checklist, route and call cadence, milestone ladder, equipment and tool recommendations, and admin-facing docs/dashboard context. Out of scope: customer-facing quote approvals, automated tax-rate engine, accounting sync, and editable internal globals until migration approval.

file.description: Dashboard, portal, admin, and quote UX planning.
Scorecard, asset ladder, milestones, and operating rhythm.
Left-rail command-center navigator and ops detail panes.
Business momentum chart with responsive metric controls.
Portal shell overflow boundaries and sidebar scroll containment.
Dashboard-specific display data and operator board content.

task.description: Keep quote scope internal-only and align field and automation changes with tax-review guidance.

Make `/ops` the internal command center with KPI cards, chart, operator panel, left-rail command navigation, daily board, scorecard, milestones, asset guidance, controlled scrolling, and runtime admin controls like CRM provider switching, while `/dashboard` stays customer-facing.
Define KPI formulas, growth thresholds, and asset/tool guidance before turning them into editable app data.

questions.question: When should the dashboard scorecard move from docs/config into an editable Payload global?
Will revenue, margin, and liability data stay manual in v1 or sync from QuickBooks later?
At what revenue or route-density threshold should larger-area commercial work become a formal target?
